steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question. The text establishes that the typical string quartet seating causes the viola's f-holes (sound openings) to be angled away from the audience. Since f-holes project sound, this reduces how clearly the audience hears the viola. During a solo, the violist wants the audience to hear clearly — so the action must address that physical constraint.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'instructs the other members of the quartet to play louder.': Having others play louder would make the viola harder to hear, not easier. This makes the solo less audible, not more. ✗
  • Choice B — 'plays at the same volume as the cellist.': Matching the cellist's volume does not solve the problem of f-holes pointing away from the audience. Volume alone cannot correct the directional acoustic issue. ✗
  • Choice C — 'shifts position in order to face outwards toward the audience.': Turning to face the audience reorients the viola so its f-holes point toward the listeners, directly solving the acoustic problem the text describes. ✓
  • Choice D — 'looks at the sheet music instead of looking at the audience.': The direction the musician looks does not change the angle of the viola's f-holes. Looking at sheet music is unrelated to the instrument's sound projection. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
